title: End User Profile And Preferences Self Service Semantic Contract
description: Source-backed draft semantic contract for the end user profile and preferences self service capability, derived from historical source-lineage evidence as lineage. Facets cite verified source-import manifest rows; proof is hermetic/runtime lineage and not fresh USF proof.
aiGuidance: Source-backed draft. Facets cite verified manifest source rows as lineage; proof is hermetic/stale and not current readiness. Do not treat as implementation authority or fresh proof; respect the hexagonal constraint in ADR 0005.

lifecycle.description: Profile lifecycle moves from a default to an updated own-profile record, and preference lifecycle moves from default-empty to updated channel/category preferences that are read back; notification dispatch then treats enabled preferences as sent and disabled preferences as suppressed. All operations act only on the authenticated subject's own data.

stateModel.description: State is tenant-and-user-scoped: a profile (display name, locale, timezone), a preference set (channel, category, enabled flag), and a durable dispatch log status of sent, suppressed, or failed. There are no secret-bearing columns in the self-service state.

permissions.description: Reads use a self-read permission and writes use a self-update permission, with the user id always taken from the authenticated session subject and never from a path or body parameter. The separate operator notification test uses platform notification permissions outside this self-service surface, preserving isolation.

contracts.description: The operation surface is read and update of the caller's own profile and notification preferences, with supporting notification readiness and test operations. Responses return the updated record or a typed validation result.

validation.description: Profile update rejects an empty display name, the route schema rejects invalid profile or preference payloads, dispatch rejects secret-bearing payload fields, and a write without tenant context returns a no-tenant error. Invalid input is rejected before any change.

errorModel.description: Validation failures return safe validation-error responses, no-tenant writes return a 400 no-tenant outcome, and secret-bearing notification payloads raise an explicit not-notifiable error; provider failure returns a typed degraded result fail-closed. No partial profile state is persisted on failure.

auditModel.description: Profile updates emit a profile-updated audit-before-change event, preference updates emit a preference audit, and operator test sends emit a notification-tested event. Audit precedes the change.

readinessModel.description: Profile and preferences are always-on once the data substrate is migrated, and degraded mode disables store-dependent writes while keeping read-only access and audit visible. Live proofs skip honestly when the substrate is unavailable and verify no secret columns plus tenant-isolation enforcement.

proof.description: Unit proofs cover the profile and notification usecases, and runtime proofs validate live profile self-service, notification preferences, and dispatch against the data substrate with row-level tenant isolation, demonstrated in source lineage via hermetic/runtime validation. Proof is hermetic/runtime lineage; fresh USF proof pending USF-101/USF-59.

uiSemanticDefinition.description: No verified UI source path in the manifest yet; author the UI semantic model or import the UI source row before generation.
